summary_en: Gakken to acquire ReaJob (TSE Standard, code 6096) as wholly-owned subsidiary via simplified stock exchange effective 2026/07/31. ReaJob shares to delist 2026/07/29. Gakken already held 20%+ stake (equity-method affiliate since Jan 2025 post-Nov 2024 capital/business tie-up). Integration to accelerate digital strategy in reskilling and English-language education segments under Gakken's mid-term plan "Gakken 2027 Value UP".
title_en: Notice of Execution of Stock Exchange Agreement for Complete Subsidiary Status of ReaJob Co., Ltd. by Gakken Holdings Co., Ltd. (Simplified Stock Exchange)
translation_en_brief:
Gakken Holdings and ReaJob executed a stock exchange agreement on 2026/05/15 to make ReaJob a wholly-owned subsidiary of Gakken effective 2026/07/31. The transaction will proceed via simplified stock exchange under Japan's Companies Act Article 796(2), requiring approval only from ReaJob shareholders at its ordinary general meeting scheduled for 2026/06/25; Gakken requires no shareholder vote. ReaJob shares will delist from TSE Standard on 2026/07/29 (final trading day 2026/07/28).

Gakken already holds over 20% of ReaJob following a capital and business tie-up agreement signed in November 2024, with ReaJob classified as an equity-method affiliate since January 2025. The rationale centers on accelerating Gakken's mid-term plan "Gakken 2027 Value UP", which prioritizes operating margin improvement and capital efficiency, with language education and reskilling as core growth pillars. Full integration will enable unrestricted data sharing, faster strategic decision-making, elimination of minority-shareholder constraints on resource reallocation, and cost synergies from consolidating redundant functions and removing public-company compliance costs.
